title: Flowchart (swimlane process flow)
description: Data contract for the flowchart format: a STRUCTURED, ORDERED BPMN-style process flowchart. Unlike the diagram-topology format (a FREE spatial node-edge graph positioned by x/y), here every node is placed deterministically by its lane (a horizontal swimlane = an actor/system) and col (its column/step ordinal); the renderer computes pixel positions from lane+col. Nodes are typed terminals (start/end), step boxes, decision diamonds, and IO parallelograms; edges are routed orthogonal SVG connectors with explicit arrowhead markers, and decision branches carry a yes/no label. The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema and is its canonical example. Render: a full-bleed pannable/zoomable canvas with a swimlane grid (one band per lane), shaped HTML nodes placed at lane/col, an inline SVG connector layer, a toolbar (zoom in/out/fit/fullscreen), a lane panel (toggle/collapse lanes), a legend, and a side info panel for the selected node.

meta.description: Shared chrome block carried by every format in the family. Brand-neutral: use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder. 'theme' picks the initial brand palette (one of the 9 presets; Black is always selectable).

org.description: Organisation wordmark shown in the bar/footer.

project.description: Project / system name shown in the title.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label, e.g. 'Process Flow', 'Approval Workflow', 'Decision Flowchart'.

title.description: Flow title shown in the top app bar.

author.description: Author / team, shown in the print footer.

theme.description: Initial brand theme; sets <html data-theme>. Default for this format is 'indigo'. Black is a mandatory, always-selectable preset.

orientation.description: Primary flow direction. 'lr' (default) lays columns left-to-right with horizontal swimlanes stacked top-to-bottom. 'tb' is reserved for top-to-bottom flows; the reference template renders 'lr'.

lanes.description: The horizontal swimlanes of the flow — each is one actor, role, or system that owns the steps placed in it (e.g. 'Applicant', 'Reviewer', 'Core System'). Lane order is top-to-bottom render order. Each lane can be toggled (hide its nodes + touching edges) and collapsed (squash its band). A node's vertical band is decided entirely by its 'lane'.

id.description: Stable lane id, referenced by node.lane.

label.description: Human lane title shown on the lane's left header band and in the lane panel.

icon.description: Optional house-icon key for the lane header (e.g. 'user','reviewer','microservice','database','approver'). Falls back to a generic glyph.

nodes.description: The flow's nodes, each placed deterministically at its (lane, col) — NOT by free x/y. 'type' decides the shape: start/end terminals (pill), step (rounded box), decision (diamond, must have outgoing yes/no branches), io (parallelogram for input/output). ~10-14 nodes across 2-3 lanes is the sweet spot.

id.description: Unique node id; referenced by edges.from / edges.to and the path-highlight logic.

type.description: Node kind → shape + role. 'start'/'end' = rounded terminals; 'step' = a process/action box; 'decision' = a diamond that branches (its outgoing edges should carry branch:'yes'|'no'); 'io' = an input/output parallelogram.

label.description: Node caption shown inside the shape.

lane.description: The owning swimlane; must match a lanes[].id. Sets the node's vertical band.

col.description: The node's column ordinal along the flow (0-based, left→right for orientation 'lr'). Sets the node's horizontal position; columns are evenly spaced. Two nodes may share a column across different lanes.

status.description: Optional state accent on the node (left edge + dot). 'neutral' is the uncoloured default.

detail.description: Optional longer description shown in the info panel and on hover (what this step does, its SLA, owner, rule, etc.).

edges.description: Directed connectors between nodes (from -> to), routed as orthogonal SVG paths with an explicit arrowhead marker. 'label' is an optional caption pill; 'branch' tags a decision's outgoing edge as the yes/no path (recolours the connector and shows a Yes/No chip).

from.description: Source node id (must match a nodes[].id).

to.description: Target node id (must match a nodes[].id).

label.description: Optional connector caption shown as a mid-path pill (e.g. an action, condition, or message).

branch.description: For an edge leaving a decision: which branch this is. 'yes' is styled as the approve/proceed path, 'no' as the reject/loop-back path.

primaryPath.description: Optional ordered list of node ids defining the happy-path / primary walkthrough that the step-through control (next/prev) advances along, centering each node. If omitted, the renderer derives a primary path by following the first/'yes' edge from the start terminal.

legend.description: Optional override of the node-type and status legend captions.

meta.title: Account Opening — Review & Approval Flow
meta.subtitle: End-to-end path from application intake through KYC, review decisions and account activation
meta.author: Research Desk

nodes.label: Application submitted
nodes.detail: Customer submits the online account-opening form with identity documents.

nodes.label: Capture KYC documents
nodes.detail: ID, proof of address and selfie are uploaded and queued for verification.

nodes.label: Automated KYC screening
nodes.detail: Sanctions, PEP and document-authenticity checks run automatically; produces a risk score.

nodes.label: Risk score acceptable?
nodes.detail: If the automated risk score is below threshold, route to fast-track; otherwise send to manual review.

nodes.label: Manual review
nodes.detail: Reviewer inspects flagged documents and the customer profile against policy. SLA: 1 business day.

nodes.label: Approve application?
nodes.detail: Reviewer decides to approve, reject, or request more information.

nodes.label: Request more info
nodes.detail: Reviewer asks the applicant for additional documentation; the case loops back to intake.

nodes.label: Fast-track approval
nodes.detail: Low-risk applications are auto-approved without manual review.

nodes.label: Open account
nodes.detail: Core banking provisions the account number, ledger entry and initial limits.

nodes.label: Notify customer
nodes.detail: Welcome pack, account details and onboarding next-steps are sent to the customer.

nodes.label: Record rejection
nodes.detail: Rejection reason is logged for audit and the applicant is informed with appeal options.

nodes.label: Account active
nodes.detail: Onboarding complete — the account is live and usable.
